properties.name: Caradene Sector
properties.description: Caradene contains several temperate worlds whose atmospheres are chemically incompatible with human life, requiring sealed settlements despite otherwise moderate climates. The independent colony of New Caradene occupies a chain of elevated habitats above a dense chlorine sea and survives through pharmaceutical exports, atmospheric chemistry, and strict control of imported population.

properties.name: Pelion Sector
properties.description: Pelion contains a loose chain of ancient red dwarfs surrounded by metal-poor rocky worlds, frozen moons, and tidally locked planets with little usable atmosphere. Several independent ice-mining settlements operate beneath the crust of Pelion Reach, supported by a rotating habitat that serves as the sector’s only dependable market and repair port.

properties.name: Far Meridian Sector
properties.description: The systems of Far Meridian are separated by unusually wide interstellar distances, leaving only two weak Guild-compatible routes through the region. A handful of independent refueling stations survive by servicing survey ships and long-range prospectors, with months of travel separating one inhabited installation from the next.

properties.name: Vesper Plain Sector
properties.description: Vesper Plain occupies a broad region of unusually low stellar density containing only scattered brown dwarfs and isolated red stars. Its systems lack concentrated mineral deposits, habitable planets, and convenient route junctions, leaving the sector as a long navigational void between more useful territories.

properties.name: Nanshi Shoals
properties.description: Dense asteroid families and partially formed planetary systems fill the Nanshi Shoals, where young stars remain surrounded by dust, rubble, and unstable orbital clusters. Independent prospectors maintain temporary camps inside larger asteroids, relocating whenever collisions, orbital shifts, or exhausted claims make their current holdings untenable.

properties.name: Tianlu Sector
properties.description: Tianlu is a young stellar region dominated by hot blue-white stars, intense ultraviolet radiation, and massive gas giants that formed before stable terrestrial worlds could develop. Automated observatories track the rapid evolution of its stars, though their data is collected remotely because long-term habitation would require shielding and maintenance costs far beyond the sector’s practical value.

properties.name: Dorian Sector
properties.description: Dorian’s low-mass stars possess numerous rocky planets, though most are too small to retain atmosphere or surface water. Independent communities inhabit sealed cylinders above the mineral world Corda, exporting precision metals through a cooperative port whose council rejects outside sovereignty and limits permanent immigration.

properties.name: Helian Waste
properties.description: Helian Waste consists of old, low-metallicity stars formed before heavier elements became common in the region. Their systems contain gas, ice, and small mineral-poor bodies, offering little material for industry and almost no planetary environments worth the cost of settlement.

properties.name: Rimeward Sector
properties.description: Rimeward is a cold region of dim stars, ice giants, frozen terrestrial worlds, and moons warmed only by tidal stress. Small independent settlements occupy geothermal caverns beneath the moon Varin, where local cooperatives harvest volatiles and biological compounds from a deep subsurface ocean.

properties.name: Cresset Sector
properties.description: Cresset contains several white dwarfs surrounded by stripped planetary cores, irradiated debris belts, and the remnants of worlds consumed during their stars’ natural expansion. The region contains valuable heavy elements, though radiation exposure, limited fuel sources, and violent debris environments have prevented permanent extraction.

properties.name: Anthelion Sector
properties.description: Anthelion is dominated by eccentric binary and trinary systems whose planets experience severe orbital variation, periodic atmospheric loss, and unpredictable bombardment from displaced debris. Surveys have identified several resource-rich worlds, but none offers a stable environment for settlements that must operate for generations rather than mining seasons.

properties.name: Lumen Verge
properties.description: Lumen Verge lies inside a thick interstellar dust cloud that obscures local stars, weakens long-range observation, and interferes with optical navigation. The few planetary systems identified within the cloud are cold and underdeveloped, with low solar output and no known worlds capable of supporting open settlement.

properties.name: Qilin Reach
properties.description: Qilin Reach contains several large ocean worlds covered beneath hundreds of kilometers of high-pressure ice. Independent research habitats operate around the moon Xiang, studying extremophile ecosystems and pressure-formed minerals while relying on imported food, machinery, and reactor fuel.

properties.name: Yanshen Sector
properties.description: A powerful magnetar dominates Yanshen, saturating the surrounding systems with periodic radiation bursts and magnetic disturbances capable of disabling insufficiently shielded vessels. No stable planets orbit close to the central star, while the outer systems contain little beyond cold gas giants and irregular moons.

properties.name: Pavan Sector
properties.description: Most Pavan systems contain gas giants, small airless moons, and little accessible heavy industry. The independent station Pavan Cross functions as a neutral transfer point for explorers, religious travelers, and long-distance freight crews, supported by docking fees rather than local production.

properties.name: Shenzhou Drift
properties.description: Shenzhou Drift consists primarily of rogue planets, failed stars, and cold objects moving between recognized systems without a common stellar center. Most bodies retain only thin frozen atmospheres or subsurface layers of exotic ice, and their changing positions make permanent lane infrastructure impractical.
